title: Add configuration option for compression level
body: Currently, compression during copying uses the default compression level. When pushing to a registry on my LAN I don't need compression, so I'd like to set `gzip.NoCompression` instead (I have a local fork that hardcodes this). Other people might want to set `gzip.BestCompression`.

comments.body: Please open a PR to do this, if you have a local patch?

This is my patch:
```patch
diff --git a/vendor/github.com/containers/image/copy/copy.go b/vendor/github.com/containers/image/copy/copy.go
index 65c3853..46a2d03 100644
--- a/vendor/github.com/containers/image/copy/copy.go
+++ b/vendor/github.com/containers/image/copy/copy.go
@@ -726,7 +726,11 @@ func compressGoroutine(dest *io.PipeWriter, src io.Reader) {
                dest.CloseWithError(err) // CloseWithError(nil) is equivalent to Close()
        }()
 
-       zipper := gzip.NewWriter(dest)
+       zipper, err := gzip.NewWriterLevel(dest, gzip.NoCompression)
+       if err != nil {
+               panic(err)
+       }
+
        defer zipper.Close()
 
        _, err = io.Copy(zipper, src) // Sets err to nil, i.e. causes dest.Close()
```
I don't think you want to apply this. I don't really know Go so I don't think I can generalize this.
@runcom @mtrmac What do you guys think of this idea?

Code-wise it would be reasonable to add a `types.SystemContext` option to c/image, and expose it as an option to `skopeo copy`.  I have no idea whether it would be used widely enough to be useful / worth the extra `--help` / man page reading time for other users; it well might.

(The compression exists not only to minimize transfer time; it’s also to decrease long-term storage costs. But again, I have no numbers and no idea how the trade-offs vs. CPU time work out.)
@mtrmac @vrothberg Should we assign this to an intern?
